different from perpendicularity there is a technical difference over here two vectors are
called orthogonal if the dot product is 0 which means even if one of the vectors
is 0 then we will say that vectors are orthogonal. However, when you talk about
perpendicularity you are talking about an angle between the two vectors and with a
zero vector the angle is not defined. So, that is why there is a technical difference
